sacm:AssuranceCasePackage.name: Open Autonomy Safety Case
sacm:AssuranceCasePackage.description: Reusable base safety case for autonomy-enabled system deployment based on Positive Trust Balance.

artifact.name: System and Deployment Description
artifact.description: Description of the Autonomous System, Deployment Context, Operational Design Domain (ODD), Operations, and Stakeholder Requirements.

artifact.name: Assurance Scope and Framework Profile
artifact.description: Selected standards, frameworks, regulatory regimes, ethical principles, and other instantiation choices for this base Safety Case.

artifact.name: Safety Management System (SMS) Claims
artifact.description: Relevant Safety Management System (SMS) Claims and associated safety management information.

artifact.name: Safety Governance Structure
artifact.description: Defined Safety Governance body or equivalent approval process for review and approval of Safety-Related Claims (SRCs).

artifact.name: Independent Assessment 13
artifact.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for Transparency.

artifact.name: Independent Assessment 14
artifact.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for Assessor Independence.

artifact.name: Independent Assessment 27
artifact.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for Safety Governance.

artifact.name: Independent Assessment 28
artifact.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for Applicable Laws and Regulations.

artifact.name: Independent Assessment 29
artifact.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for Applicable Ethical Principles.

artifact.name: Independent Assessment 30
artifact.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for the Selected Safety Management Framework.

artifact.name: Independent Assessment 31
artifact.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for UL 4600 Continuous Improvement.

artifact.name: Independent Assessment 32
artifact.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for the Selected Quality Management Framework.

artifact.name: Independent Assessment 61
artifact.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for the Selected Hazard Analysis Method.

artifact.name: Independent Assessment 62
artifact.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for Applicable Regulatory and Approval Requirements.

artifact.name: Independent Assessment 63
artifact.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for ISO 26262 conformance.

artifact.name: Independent Assessment 64
artifact.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for ISO/PAS 8800 conformance.

artifact.name: Independent Assessment 65
artifact.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for ISO 21448 conformance.

artifact.name: Independent Assessment 66
artifact.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for the Selected Cybersecurity Standard.

artifact.name: Independent Assessment 67
artifact.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for the Selected Configuration Management Standard.

artifact.name: Independent Assessment 68
artifact.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for the Selected Data Recording Guidance.

artifact.name: Independent Assessment 80
artifact.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for Trip Interruptions.

artifact.name: Independent Assessment 81
artifact.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for Post-Incident Response.

artifact.name: Independent Assessment 82
artifact.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for Remote Operations.

artifact.name: Independent Assessment 83
artifact.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for First Responder Interaction.

artifact.name: Independent Assessment 84
artifact.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for Safety Inspections.

artifact.name: Independent Assessment 85
artifact.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for Safety Metrics monitoring.

artifact.name: Independent Assessment 86
artifact.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for Operating Procedures that mitigate Residual Hazards.

argumentPackage.name: Open Autonomy Safety Case - Main Argument

claim.name: Goal 1
claim.content: The Autonomous System is acceptably safe for its Intended Deployment.
claim.description: Top-level safety claim for deployment of the Autonomous System.

claim.name: Goal 3
claim.content: The Organization demonstrates Organizational Trustworthiness.
claim.description: Organizational trustworthiness claim for the Autonomous System lifecycle.

claim.name: Goal 4
claim.content: The Autonomous System is acceptably safe within the Operational Design Domain (ODD).
claim.description: Autonomous System safety claim for the defined Operational Design Domain (ODD).

claim.name: Goal 5
claim.content: Operations of the Autonomous System are acceptably safe within the Operational Design Domain (ODD).
claim.description: Operational Safety claim for the defined Operations and Operational Design Domain (ODD).

argumentReasoning.name: Strategy 2
argumentReasoning.content: Appeal to Positive Trust Balance across Organizational Trustworthiness, Autonomous System safety, and Operational Safety.
argumentReasoning.description: Top-level strategy based on Positive Trust Balance across organization, system, and operations.

artifactReference.name: System and Deployment Context
artifactReference.description: Description of the Autonomous System, Deployment Context, Operational Design Domain (ODD), Operations, and Stakeholder Requirements.

artifactReference.name: Assurance Scope Context
artifactReference.description: Selected standards, frameworks, regulatory regimes, and ethical principles for instantiating this base Safety Case.

assertedInference.name: Top-Level Inference
assertedInference.description: The top-level claim is supported by Organizational Trustworthiness, Autonomous System safety, and Operational Safety through Positive Trust Balance.

assertedContext.name: Top-Level Context
assertedContext.description: System and Deployment Context informs the Positive Trust Balance strategy.

assertedContext.name: Assurance Scope Context Link
assertedContext.description: Assurance Scope and Framework Profile informs the Positive Trust Balance strategy and selected instantiation choices.

claim.name: Goal 8
claim.content: Results of Independent Safety Assessment are regularly shared with Stakeholders.
claim.description: Transparency through sharing results of Independent Safety Assessment.

claim.name: Goal 9
claim.content: Assessor Independence is sufficient for the Assurance Objectives.
claim.description: Independent assessor qualification and independence are justified for the Assurance Objectives.

claim.name: Goal 10
claim.content: Safety Management System (SMS) policies are aligned with Stakeholder Requirements and the Deployment Context.
claim.description: Safety Management System (SMS) policies align with Stakeholder Requirements and the Deployment Context.

claim.name: Goal 11
claim.content: Safety Management System (SMS) processes are defined, deployed, and used.
claim.description: Safety Management System (SMS) processes are actively implemented.

claim.name: Goal 12
claim.content: The Safety Case is monitored and improved as part of Continuous Improvement.
claim.description: Continuous monitoring and improvement of the Safety Case.

claim.name: Goal 21
claim.content: Safety-Related Claims (SRCs) are reviewed and approved through Safety Governance.
claim.description: Safety-Related Claims (SRCs) are approved through a defined governance body or equivalent process.

claim.name: Goal 22
claim.content: Safety-Related Claims (SRCs) satisfy Applicable Laws and Regulations for the Deployment Context.
claim.description: Safety-Related Claims (SRCs) satisfy deployment-specific laws, regulations, and approval conditions.

claim.name: Goal 23
claim.content: Safety-Related Claims (SRCs) are aligned with Applicable Ethical Principles for the Deployment Context.
claim.description: Safety-Related Claims (SRCs) are aligned with applicable ethical principles and organization-adopted ethical commitments.

claim.name: Goal 24
claim.content: The Safety Management System (SMS) conforms with the Selected Safety Management Framework.
claim.description: Safety Management System (SMS) conformance to a selected management framework appropriate to the Deployment Context.

claim.name: Goal 25
claim.content: The Safety Case is continually improved in conformance with UL 4600.
claim.description: Continuous improvement aligned with UL 4600.

claim.name: Goal 26
claim.content: The Organization conforms with the Selected Quality Management Framework.
claim.description: Organizational quality management framework conformance. Example instantiation: IATF 16949.

argumentReasoning.name: Strategy 6
argumentReasoning.content: Appeal to Transparency and Independent Safety Assessment.
argumentReasoning.description: Argument that Transparency through Independent Safety Assessment builds trust.

argumentReasoning.name: Strategy 7
argumentReasoning.content: Appeal to systematic Safety Management System (SMS) implementation.
argumentReasoning.description: Argument that disciplined Safety Management System (SMS) implementation supports trustworthiness.

artifactReference.name: Governance Context
artifactReference.description: Defined Safety Governance body or equivalent process responsible for review and approval of Safety-Related Claims (SRCs).

argumentReasoning.name: Strategy 15
argumentReasoning.content: Appeal to Safety Governance.
argumentReasoning.description: Argument based on defined Safety Governance for review and approval of Safety-Related Claims (SRCs).

argumentReasoning.name: Strategy 16
argumentReasoning.content: Appeal to Applicable Laws and Regulations.
argumentReasoning.description: Argument based on compliance with Applicable Laws and Regulations.

argumentReasoning.name: Strategy 17
argumentReasoning.content: Appeal to Applicable Ethical Principles.
argumentReasoning.description: Argument based on applicable ethical principles for the Deployment Context.

argumentReasoning.name: Strategy 18
argumentReasoning.content: Appeal to the Selected Safety Management Framework.
argumentReasoning.description: Argument based on a selected safety management framework appropriate to the Deployment Context.

argumentReasoning.name: Strategy 19
argumentReasoning.content: Appeal to UL 4600.
argumentReasoning.description: Argument based on UL 4600.

argumentReasoning.name: Strategy 20
argumentReasoning.content: Appeal to the Selected Quality Management Framework.
argumentReasoning.description: Argument based on a selected quality management framework. Example instantiation: IATF 16949.

artifactReference.name: Evidence 13
artifactReference.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for Transparency.

artifactReference.name: Evidence 14
artifactReference.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for Assessor Independence.

artifactReference.name: Evidence 27
artifactReference.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for Safety Governance.

artifactReference.name: Evidence 28
artifactReference.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for Applicable Laws and Regulations.

artifactReference.name: Evidence 29
artifactReference.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for Applicable Ethical Principles.

artifactReference.name: Evidence 30
artifactReference.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for the Selected Safety Management Framework.

artifactReference.name: Evidence 31
artifactReference.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for UL 4600 Continuous Improvement.

artifactReference.name: Evidence 32
artifactReference.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for the Selected Quality Management Framework.

assertedInference.name: Transparency Inference
assertedInference.description: Transparency and Assessor Independence support Organizational Trustworthiness.

assertedInference.name: SMS Inference
assertedInference.description: Safety Management System (SMS) policies, processes, and Continuous Improvement support Organizational Trustworthiness.

assertedInference.name: Advisory Board Approval Inference
assertedInference.description: Safety Governance supports alignment of Safety Management System (SMS) policies.

assertedContext.name: Advisory Board Context
assertedContext.description: Governance Context informs the Safety Governance strategy.

assertedInference.name: Regulatory Compliance Inference
assertedInference.description: Applicable Laws and Regulations support alignment of Safety Management System (SMS) policies.

assertedInference.name: Ethical Principles Inference
assertedInference.description: Applicable Ethical Principles support alignment of Safety Management System (SMS) policies.

assertedInference.name: Safety Management Framework Inference
assertedInference.description: The Selected Safety Management Framework supports Safety Management System (SMS) processes.

assertedInference.name: UL 4600 Improvement Inference
assertedInference.description: UL 4600 supports Continuous Improvement of the Safety Case.

assertedInference.name: IATF 16949 Inference
assertedInference.description: The Selected Quality Management Framework supports Continuous Improvement of the Safety Case.

assertedEvidence.name: Transparency Evidence
assertedEvidence.description: Independent Safety Assessment results support the Transparency claim.

assertedEvidence.name: Assessor Independence Evidence
assertedEvidence.description: Independent Safety Assessment results support the Assessor Independence claim.

assertedEvidence.name: Advisory Board Approval Evidence
assertedEvidence.description: Independent Safety Assessment results support the Safety Governance claim.

assertedEvidence.name: Regulatory Compliance Evidence
assertedEvidence.description: Independent Safety Assessment results support the Applicable Laws and Regulations claim.

assertedEvidence.name: Ethical Principles Evidence
assertedEvidence.description: Independent Safety Assessment results support the Applicable Ethical Principles claim.

assertedEvidence.name: Safety Management Framework Evidence
assertedEvidence.description: Independent Safety Assessment results support the Selected Safety Management Framework claim.

assertedEvidence.name: UL 4600 Improvement Evidence
assertedEvidence.description: Independent Safety Assessment results support the UL 4600 Continuous Improvement claim.

assertedEvidence.name: IATF 16949 Evidence
assertedEvidence.description: Independent Safety Assessment results support the Selected Quality Management Framework claim.

claim.name: Goal 37
claim.content: Hazard Analysis covers all reasonably foreseeable hazards posed by the Autonomous System.
claim.description: Coverage of reasonably foreseeable hazards by Hazard Analysis.

claim.name: Goal 38
claim.content: The Autonomous System conforms with Applicable Regulatory and Approval Requirements.
claim.description: Conformance with applicable regulatory and approval requirements for the Deployment Context.

claim.name: Goal 39
claim.content: The Autonomous System satisfies Functional Safety requirements.
claim.description: Functional Safety requirements are satisfied. Example instantiation: ISO 26262.

claim.name: Goal 40
claim.content: The Autonomous System is developed with appropriate AI / Autonomy Engineering Rigor.
claim.description: AI / Autonomy Engineering Rigor is justified. Example instantiation: ISO/PAS 8800.

claim.name: Goal 41
claim.content: The Safety of the Intended Functionality (SOTIF) of the Autonomous System is acceptable.
claim.description: Safety of the Intended Functionality (SOTIF) is justified. Example instantiation: ISO 21448.

claim.name: Goal 42
claim.content: The Autonomous System conforms with applicable Cybersecurity requirements.
claim.description: Conformance with applicable Cybersecurity requirements. Example instantiation: ISO 21434.

claim.name: Goal 43
claim.content: Configuration Management is applied to safety-relevant items and changes.
claim.description: Configuration Management practices are applied to safety-relevant items and changes.

claim.name: Goal 44
claim.content: Data Recording supports safety monitoring, incident investigation, and learning.
claim.description: Data Recording supports safety monitoring, incident investigation, and learning.

claim.name: Goal 53
claim.content: A Hazard Analysis has been completed using the Selected Hazard Analysis Method.
claim.description: Hazard Analysis performed using a selected method. Example instantiation: SAE J3307 / STPA.

claim.name: Goal 54
claim.content: Applicable Regulatory and Approval Requirements are satisfied.
claim.description: Applicable regulatory and approval requirements are satisfied.

claim.name: Goal 55
claim.content: The Autonomous System is developed according to ISO 26262 or an equivalent justified Functional Safety process.
claim.description: Development aligned with ISO 26262 or an equivalent justified Functional Safety process.

claim.name: Goal 56
claim.content: The Autonomous System is developed according to ISO/PAS 8800 or an equivalent justified AI / Autonomy Safety process.
claim.description: Development aligned with ISO/PAS 8800 or an equivalent justified AI / Autonomy Safety process.

claim.name: Goal 57
claim.content: The Autonomous System is developed according to ISO 21448 or an equivalent justified intended functionality safety process.
claim.description: Development aligned with ISO 21448 or an equivalent justified intended functionality safety process.

claim.name: Goal 58
claim.content: The Autonomous System is developed according to the Selected Cybersecurity Standard or an equivalent justified process.
claim.description: Development aligned with a selected Cybersecurity standard. Example instantiation: ISO 21434.

claim.name: Goal 59
claim.content: Configuration Management is performed according to the Selected Configuration Management Standard or an equivalent justified process.
claim.description: Configuration Management aligned with a selected standard. Example instantiation: ISO 10007.

claim.name: Goal 60
claim.content: Data Recording for the Autonomous System and Operations conforms with the Selected Data Recording Guidance.
claim.description: Data Recording aligned with selected guidance. Example instantiation: SAE AVSC 00004.

argumentReasoning.name: Strategy 33
argumentReasoning.content: Appeal to Hazard Analysis.
argumentReasoning.description: Argument based on Hazard Analysis methods.

argumentReasoning.name: Strategy 34
argumentReasoning.content: Appeal to risk reduction through Applicable Regulatory and Approval Requirements, Functional Safety, AI / Autonomy Engineering Rigor, Safety of the Intended Functionality (SOTIF), and Cybersecurity.
argumentReasoning.description: Argument based on risk reduction through standards, requirements, and justified engineering rigor.

argumentReasoning.name: Strategy 35
argumentReasoning.content: Appeal to Configuration Management.
argumentReasoning.description: Argument based on Configuration Management.

argumentReasoning.name: Strategy 36
argumentReasoning.content: Appeal to Data Recording and forensic readiness.
argumentReasoning.description: Argument based on Data Recording for incident investigation and learning.

argumentReasoning.name: Strategy 45
argumentReasoning.content: Appeal to the Selected Hazard Analysis Method.
argumentReasoning.description: Argument based on the selected Hazard Analysis method. Example instantiation: SAE J3307 / STPA.

argumentReasoning.name: Strategy 46
argumentReasoning.content: Appeal to Applicable Regulatory and Approval Requirements.
argumentReasoning.description: Argument based on applicable regulatory and approval requirements for the Deployment Context.

argumentReasoning.name: Strategy 47
argumentReasoning.content: Appeal to ISO 26262.
argumentReasoning.description: Argument based on ISO 26262.

argumentReasoning.name: Strategy 48
argumentReasoning.content: Appeal to ISO/PAS 8800.
argumentReasoning.description: Argument based on ISO/PAS 8800.

argumentReasoning.name: Strategy 49
argumentReasoning.content: Appeal to ISO 21448.
argumentReasoning.description: Argument based on ISO 21448.

argumentReasoning.name: Strategy 50
argumentReasoning.content: Appeal to the Selected Cybersecurity Standard.
argumentReasoning.description: Argument based on the selected Cybersecurity standard. Example instantiation: ISO 21434.

argumentReasoning.name: Strategy 51
argumentReasoning.content: Appeal to the Selected Configuration Management Standard.
argumentReasoning.description: Argument based on the selected Configuration Management standard. Example instantiation: ISO 10007.

argumentReasoning.name: Strategy 52
argumentReasoning.content: Appeal to the Selected Data Recording Guidance.
argumentReasoning.description: Argument based on the selected Data Recording guidance. Example instantiation: SAE AVSC 00004.

artifactReference.name: Evidence 61
artifactReference.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for the Selected Hazard Analysis Method.

artifactReference.name: Evidence 62
artifactReference.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for Applicable Regulatory and Approval Requirements.

artifactReference.name: Evidence 63
artifactReference.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for ISO 26262 conformance.

artifactReference.name: Evidence 64
artifactReference.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for ISO/PAS 8800 conformance.

artifactReference.name: Evidence 65
artifactReference.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for ISO 21448 conformance.

artifactReference.name: Evidence 66
artifactReference.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for the Selected Cybersecurity Standard.

artifactReference.name: Evidence 67
artifactReference.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for the Selected Configuration Management Standard.

artifactReference.name: Evidence 68
artifactReference.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for the Selected Data Recording Guidance.

assertedInference.name: Hazard Analysis Inference
assertedInference.description: Hazard Analysis supports Autonomous System safety.

assertedInference.name: Risk Mitigation Inference
assertedInference.description: Requirements conformance and justified engineering rigor support Autonomous System safety through risk reduction.

assertedInference.name: Configuration Management Inference
assertedInference.description: Configuration Management supports Autonomous System safety.

assertedInference.name: Data Recording Inference
assertedInference.description: Data Recording supports Autonomous System safety through monitoring and learning.

assertedInference.name: STPA Inference
assertedInference.description: The Selected Hazard Analysis Method supports the Hazard Analysis claim.

assertedInference.name: Regulatory Requirements Inference
assertedInference.description: Applicable Regulatory and Approval Requirements support the corresponding claim.

assertedInference.name: ISO 26262 Inference
assertedInference.description: ISO 26262 supports the Functional Safety claim.

assertedInference.name: ISO/PAS 8800 Inference
assertedInference.description: ISO/PAS 8800 supports the AI / Autonomy Engineering Rigor claim.

assertedInference.name: ISO 21448 Inference
assertedInference.description: ISO 21448 supports the SOTIF claim.

assertedInference.name: ISO 21434 Inference
assertedInference.description: The Selected Cybersecurity Standard supports the Cybersecurity claim.

assertedInference.name: ISO 10007 Inference
assertedInference.description: The Selected Configuration Management Standard supports the Configuration Management claim.

assertedInference.name: SAE AVSC 00004 Inference
assertedInference.description: The Selected Data Recording Guidance supports the Data Recording claim.

assertedEvidence.name: STPA Evidence
assertedEvidence.description: Independent Safety Assessment results support the Selected Hazard Analysis Method claim.

assertedEvidence.name: Regulatory Requirements Evidence
assertedEvidence.description: Independent Safety Assessment results support the Applicable Regulatory and Approval Requirements claim.

assertedEvidence.name: ISO 26262 Evidence
assertedEvidence.description: Independent Safety Assessment results support the ISO 26262 claim.

assertedEvidence.name: ISO/PAS 8800 Evidence
assertedEvidence.description: Independent Safety Assessment results support the ISO/PAS 8800 claim.

assertedEvidence.name: ISO 21448 Evidence
assertedEvidence.description: Independent Safety Assessment results support the ISO 21448 claim.

assertedEvidence.name: ISO 21434 Evidence
assertedEvidence.description: Independent Safety Assessment results support the Selected Cybersecurity Standard claim.

assertedEvidence.name: ISO 10007 Evidence
assertedEvidence.description: Independent Safety Assessment results support the Selected Configuration Management Standard claim.

assertedEvidence.name: SAE AVSC 00004 Evidence
assertedEvidence.description: Independent Safety Assessment results support the Selected Data Recording Guidance claim.

claim.name: Goal 72
claim.content: Operations support safe management of Trip Interruptions.
claim.description: Trip interruption management. Example instantiation: SAE AVSC 00003.

claim.name: Goal 73
claim.content: Operations support safe Post-Incident Response.
claim.description: Post-incident response. Example instantiation: SAE AVSC-I-05 post-crash operations.

claim.name: Goal 74
claim.content: Remote Operations are defined and controlled.
claim.description: Remote Operations guidance. Example instantiation: SAE AVSC-I-04.

claim.name: Goal 75
claim.content: First Responder Interaction procedures are defined and communicated.
claim.description: First Responder Interaction procedures. Example instantiation: SAE AVSC-I-01.

claim.name: Goal 76
claim.content: Safety Inspections are defined and performed.
claim.description: Safety Inspection procedures. Example instantiation: SAE AVSC-D-03.

claim.name: Goal 77
claim.content: Safety Metrics are monitored during Operations.
claim.description: Operational Safety metrics monitoring. Example instantiation: SAE AVSC 00006.

claim.name: Goal 78
claim.content: Operating Procedures mitigate Residual Hazards not fully controlled by technical means.
claim.description: Procedural mitigation of Residual Hazards.

argumentReasoning.name: Strategy 69
argumentReasoning.content: Appeal to applicable Operational Safety practices.
argumentReasoning.description: Argument based on operational safety practices and guidance.

argumentReasoning.name: Strategy 70
argumentReasoning.content: Appeal to Operating Procedures for Residual Hazards.
argumentReasoning.description: Argument that Operating Procedures address Residual Hazards.

artifactReference.name: Safety Management Context
artifactReference.description: Relevant Safety Management System (SMS) Claims providing context for Operational Safety.

artifactReference.name: Evidence 80
artifactReference.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for Trip Interruptions.

artifactReference.name: Evidence 81
artifactReference.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for Post-Incident Response.

artifactReference.name: Evidence 82
artifactReference.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for Remote Operations.

artifactReference.name: Evidence 83
artifactReference.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for First Responder Interaction.

artifactReference.name: Evidence 84
artifactReference.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for Safety Inspections.

artifactReference.name: Evidence 85
artifactReference.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for Safety Metrics monitoring.

artifactReference.name: Evidence 86
artifactReference.description: Results of Independent Safety Assessment for Operating Procedures that mitigate Residual Hazards.

assertedInference.name: Operational Best Practices Inference
assertedInference.description: Operational safety practices support safe Operations of the Autonomous System.

assertedInference.name: Procedural Mitigations Inference
assertedInference.description: Operating Procedures support safe Operations of the Autonomous System.

assertedContext.name: SMS Context for Operational Safety Strategy
assertedContext.description: Safety Management System (SMS) Claims provide context for the Operational Safety strategy.

assertedEvidence.name: Trip Interruption Evidence
assertedEvidence.description: Independent Safety Assessment results support the Trip Interruptions claim.

assertedEvidence.name: Post-Crash Evidence
assertedEvidence.description: Independent Safety Assessment results support the Post-Incident Response claim.

assertedEvidence.name: Remote Operations Evidence
assertedEvidence.description: Independent Safety Assessment results support the Remote Operations claim.

assertedEvidence.name: First Responder Evidence
assertedEvidence.description: Independent Safety Assessment results support the First Responder Interaction claim.

assertedEvidence.name: Safety Inspections Evidence
assertedEvidence.description: Independent Safety Assessment results support the Safety Inspections claim.

assertedEvidence.name: Metrics Monitoring Evidence
assertedEvidence.description: Independent Safety Assessment results support the Safety Metrics claim.

assertedEvidence.name: Procedural Mitigations Evidence
assertedEvidence.description: Independent Safety Assessment results support the Operating Procedures claim.
